concerns.text: The plan states 'No power analysis is possible' and relies on 'raw rates' with a '±5% tolerance' for comparison. This approach lacks statistical validity for claiming reproduction. Without a defined effect size or confidence intervals, a ±5% difference could be statistically significant or noise depending on sample size. The methodology must define how 'reproduction' is statistically validated (e.g., equivalence testing, confidence interval overlap) rather than a fixed arbitrary tolerance.

The plan assumes the test set is 'small enough' or uses a '--limit' flag for partial runs. If the reproduction relies on a subset (sampling) without a defined sampling strategy (e.g., stratified, random), the resulting metrics may not be representative of the full benchmark, invalidating the comparison to the paper's full-dataset results. The methodology must specify the sampling method or commit to processing the full test set.

The plan targets '≥ 90% of test videos' processing success. If 10% of samples are dropped due to timeouts or errors, the calculated failure-mode rates (PR, CR, etc.) will be biased if the dropped samples are not random (e.g., if longer/harder videos time out). The methodology must address how non-random dropouts affect the validity of the computed metrics or ensure the dropout rate is negligible.
